---
title: "5.3.2 Keeping data safe from security threats"
subtitle: "Two-step verification and automatic software updates · P204-P205"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.72em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## Two-step Verification

### Two methods of authentication

<span class="term">Two-step verification</span> (两步验证) requires two methods of authentication to verify who a user is.

It is used predominantly when a user makes an online purchase using a credit/debit card as payment method.

## Purchase Example

### Step 1

Suppose Kate wishes to buy a new camera from a website.

She logs into the website using her computer. This requires her to enter a <span class="term">user name</span> and a <span class="term">password</span>, which is step 1 of the authentication process.

## Purchase Example

### Step 2

To improve security, an eight-digit PIN, called a <span class="term">one-time pass code</span>, is sent back to her either in an email or as a text message to her mobile phone.

Kate enters this eight-digit PIN into her computer and is now authorised to buy the camera.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.17 Two-step verification using a mobile phone screenshot placeholder
:::

## Authentication Factors

### Something she has and knows

Using the textbook definitions:

::: {.tight-list}
- the mobile phone is <span class="mark">something she has</span>
- the password/PIN code is <span class="mark">something she knows</span>
:::

## Automatic Software Updates

### Keeping software current

<span class="term">Automatic software updates</span> mean software on computers and mobile phones/tablets is kept up-to-date.

Sometimes this is done overnight or when the user logs off the device.

## Why Updates Matter

### Patches and performance

These updates are vital since they may contain <span class="term">patches</span> that:

::: {.tight-list}
- update the software security to protect against malware
- improve software performance
- remove bugs
- add new features
:::

The downside is the potential for updates to disrupt a device following installation.

## Update Problems

### Waiting or reversing

If updates disrupt the device, the user either has to wait for another patch or use techniques from Chapter 4 that reverse the clock time to an earlier date before the updates were made.

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.18 Automatic software update flow chart screenshot placeholder
:::

## Classroom Check

### Two-step verification and updates

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- two-step verification uses two authentication methods
- the first step can be user name and password
- the second step can be a one-time pass code sent to email or mobile phone
- the mobile phone is something the user has; the password/PIN is something the user knows
- automatic updates keep software up-to-date and may include security patches
- updates can disrupt the device after installation
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Email and URL checks

The next deck explains clues that identify scam emails, then moves to firewalls and proxy servers.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
